messages.content: 你好，现在你是一名英语老师，做过许多英语阅读题，现在我要问你几道阅读题
你好！既然我现在是一名英语老师的角色，我很乐意帮助你解答英语阅读题。请告诉我你的问题或者提供阅读材料以及相关的问题，我将尽我的最大能力来帮助你。

The Hippocratic Oath(希波克拉底誓言，即医生从业前所立的保证遵守医德、拯救生命的誓言)is taken by doctors and other healthcare professionals swearing topractice medicine ethically(合乎道德地)and promising not to do more harm thangood when treating a patient.
it's a shame there isn't a similar oath taken by government leaders, bankers.brokers, traders,and regulators, among many others too. Clearly the financialmarkets are in need of a refresher course in the subject of ethics.
'm in New York this week to consult with a law firm regarding the MF (short forMan Financial) Global case. They hired me to help them analyze the situation andadvise them.In my opinion what MF Global, and in turn the CME Group andCommodity Futures Trading Commission have done is a truly betrayal of customertrust and their moral obligations.
've also had the opportunity to walk around Wall Street, where  chatted with theOccupy Wall Street folks. and really listened to their stories.
 talked to many people who have been deeply impacted by the poor decisionsmade by government and business leaders, reckless regulators, and corruptbanks.
A wide array of once-trusted and respected institutions have shown a generadisreaard for basic ethics and moral obligations, thereby undermining the public'strust.and confidence.You reap what vou sow.
Many of the media reports say that the vast majority of Occupy Wall Streetprotestors are a lost generation of“hippies”(嬉皮) out for free love and aimlessprotest. But from what I can see, that's way off base. What we have are a lot ofpeople who feel deprived of the right to vote for the American Dream, which hasnow turned into the American nightmare for many.
The fact of the matter is that the protests may not be well organized. But theunderlying distrust of financial and government institutions has spread to all levelsof the socioeconomic scale. and the protests reflect that.
The “99 percent” as the protestors call themselves, are angry over foreclosures (取消抵押品赎回权)and lack of due diligence (应有的注意)by banks,lack of a properhealthcare system by a broken government, and an almost nonexistent jobmarket. The list goes on and on.
这是原文，请你回答我接下来问你的问题
1. What can be inferred about Hippocratic Oath according
to the passage?
AMedical professionals should take Hippocratic
Oath to follow their ethics.
B.Hippocratic Oath is taken by patients to protectthemselves from any harm.
C.We need to refresh the financial markets by taking
the Hippocratic Oath.
D.People in government departments should alsotake Hippocratic Oath.
2.What does the author think of the MF Global?
A.MF Global betrayed its customers because of thepoor decision made by the CME Group and CommodityFutures Trading Commission.
BMF Global has been deeply impacted by the poordecisions made by government and business leadersC.MF Global got what it deserved because it didn'tstick to its professional ethics.
D.MF Global should have had a Hippocratic Oath to
take
3.What does the author mean by saying“You reap whatyou sow”(Para.6)?
A. The author has found what is undermining thepublic's trust,and confidence.
B.Many institutions have lost the public trust andconfidence due to their disregard for basic ethics andmoral obligations.
C.The Wall Street protestors cannot realize theirAmerican Dream due to their lack of aim in their life.
D.People have been greatly influenced by the poordecisions made by government.
4.Why has the American Dream turned out to be Americannightmare for many people?
A. Because people don't trust financial andgovernment institutions any more.
 B. Because they have been deprived of the right tovote against government.
C. Because they haven't been well organized in theirprotests against government and business leadersD.Because they don't have basic ethics and moralobligations.
5. Which of the following is true according to the passage?A. The vast majority of Occupy Wall Street protestorsare a lost generation of "hippies" out for free love andaimless protest.
B. Wall Street, Congress, banks and financialinstitutions haven't found their moral compass for quite aong time.
C.Government leaders, bankers, brokers, tradersand regulators have taken the Hippocratic OathD. Congress, banks and financial institutions havedone whatever they can to monitor the economicactivities in the Wall Street.
这五道题选什么？

根据提供的文段，下面是我对各问题的推断和答案：

1. What can be inferred about Hippocratic Oath according to the passage?
答案: A. Medical professionals should take Hippocratic Oath to follow their ethics.
解析: 文段提到医生们宣誓遵守医德，因此可以推断希波克拉底誓言是医疗专业人员关于遵循职业道德的誓言，而非病人的誓言。

2. What does the author think of the MF Global?
答案: A. MF Global betrayed its customers because of the poor decision made by the CME Group and Commodity Futures Trading Commission.
解析: 作者明确表示认为MF Global、CME Group和Commodity Futures Trading Commission背叛了顾客信任和他们的道德义务，因此选项A符合作者的观点。

3. What does the author mean by saying “You reap what you sow” (Para.6)?
答案: B. Many institutions have lost the public trust and confidence due to their disregard for basic ethics and moral obligations.
解析: 在上下文中，该句话是指由于很多机构忽视基本的伦理道德，所以失去了公众的信任和信心。

4. Why has the American Dream turned out to be American nightmare for many people?
答案: A. Because people don't trust financial and government institutions any more.
解析: 文段提到抗议者对金融和政府机构不信任，以及有关住房拍卖、健康系统不完善和就业市场几乎消失等问题，这表明人们之所以经历“美国梦”的噩梦是因为他们失去了对这些机构的信任。

5. Which of the following is true according to the passage?
答案: B. Wall Street, Congress, banks and financial institutions haven't found their moral compass for quite a long time.
解析: 依据文段，许多曾经受人尊敬的机构显示出对伦理道德的普遍漠视，因此B选项准确地概括了作者对金融机构和政府机构的看法。其他选项要么与文段信息不符，要么是错误信息。

How many ways are there to say “No” without offending anyone's feeling? Wellthere are “No, I can't”,“No, l don't have time" and “No, I don't want to”. But theproblem is that many of us try to avoid situations that require us to say “No” topeople. In almost every culture this little word is associated with rejection, failureegoism and a lack of tact and empathy (感同身受)towards others.
cannot recall how many times l have eaten burnt,undercooked,bland(淡而无味) and poorly tasting dishes simply because I did not want to hurt the feelings ofthe person who had cooked them. Or the times when I bought something l did notneed, because  felt quilty leaving a shop empty-handed after spending 20 minutesof the shop assistant's time.
Whether it is our manager, a customer service representative or an acquaintanceasking for a favor, for some unexplainable reason many of us feel mentaresistance when we have to voice a straight-forward “No”
f you are a rare exception, who has no problem saying “l wish I could, but l do notwant to" please share your secret! What is running through your head when an 8year old with huge blue eves offers you to buy a box of Girl Scouts cookies? Owhen vour boss asks you in a matter-of-fact voice if vou can stay after hours tofinish a project?
How do you say “No” without offending anyone or feeling quilty afterwards?
On the other hand, how do you NOT say “No” when you know that if you go alongwith everything other people want from you, you would be physically, mentally andemotionally exhausted?
I believe the answer to this question lies in the wisdom of the Eastern world. Yousee, many oriental cultures, Chinese being one of them, do not even have a “N”word. Instead, they use thousands of ways to express their disagreement andnone of them involves saying a straight-forward “No”
There is a great lesson to be learned from such approach to communication.
lf just like me, you sometimes find it difficult to be direct about saying “No", youcan still be assertive and express your disagreement in a more subtle, yet equallypowerful way. The great thing about this method is that it gets your point acrosswithout making you look bad, unprofessional, insensitive or uncaring.
这是原文，请你回答接下来的几道问题
6.What does saying“No" mean in most cultures accordingto the passage?
A. lt is associated with the sense of failure andgetting hurt.
B. It is a mild word without offending anyone's
feeling.
C. It helps us avoid situations that we may get hurt.
D.It suggests refusal and self-centeredness towards
others.
7. Which of the following statement is true about the authoraccording to the passage?
A. The author felt guilty because he/she boughtnothing when leaving a shop
B. The author has refused the boss' suggestion tooverwork many times.
C.The author bought a box of cookies from an 8-year old child.
D. The author has eaten many poorly cooked dishes8.Why should we learn from the wisdom of communicationapproach in the Eastern world?
A. Because people there seldom offend others'feelings.
O B. Because people there usually express theirdisagreement in a subtle way
C.Because there is no word meaning “no"in manyoriental cultures.
D.Because there are many ways to express theirdisagreement more powerfully.
9. Which of the following is closest in meaning to the phraseget your point across”(Para.9)?
A. Explain your point carefully.
B Put forward your point.
C.Accept your view.
D. Make yourself understood.
10. Which of the following cannot be inferred from thepassage?
A.People often feel mental resistance when theyhave to say“No
B. People often feel guilty after they have said “No'directly.
C.Avoiding expressing disagreement is a goodstrategy
D.People are reluctant to say “No” because theydon't want to offend others
请你回答一下这五道题

根据提供的文段，以下是我对这五个问题的回答：

6. What does saying "No" mean in most cultures according to the passage?
答案: D. It suggests refusal and self-centeredness towards others.
解析: 文段中提到这个简短的单词通常被关联于拒绝、失败、自我中心以及缺乏对他人的体贴和共情。

7. Which of the following statement is true about the author according to the passage?
答案: D. The author has eaten many poorly cooked dishes.
解析: 作者提到因为不想伤害做饭的人的感情，吃了很多次不好吃的饭菜，这表明了真实发生过的事情选项D正确。

8. Why should we learn from the wisdom of communication approach in the Eastern world?
答案: B. Because people there usually express their disagreement in a subtle way.
解析: 作者认为东方世界的智慧在于他们使用成千上万的方式来表达不同意见，而这些方式并不涉及直接说“No”。

9. Which of the following is closest in meaning to the phrase "get your point across" (Para.9)?
答案: D. Make yourself understood.
解析: “Get your point across”意味着让别人理解你的观点或意见，因此答案D“Make yourself understood”是最接近的意思。

10. Which of the following cannot be inferred from the passage?
答案: C. Avoiding expressing disagreement is a good strategy.
解析: 文中没有表明避免表达不同意见是一个好策略，反而是在讨论如何以一种恰当的方式来表达这种不同意见。其他选项A、B和D都是文中可以推断出来的。

In today's knowledge economy, nations survive on the things they do bestJapanese design electronics while Germens export engineering techniques. TheFrench serve the best food and Americans make computers.
Britain specializes in the gift of talking. The nation doesn't manufacture much otanything. But it has lawyers, stylists and business consultants who earn their livincfrom talk, talk and more talk. The World Foundation think tank (智囊库) savs theUK's four iconic jobs today are not scientists, engineers, teachers and nursesnstead, they're hairdressers, celebrities, management consultants and managersBut can all this talking keep the British economy going? The British governmentthinks it can.
Although the country's trade deficit was more than f 60 billion in 2006. UK'argest in the postwar period, officials say the country has nothing to worry aboutn fact Britain does have a world-class pharmaceutical (制药的)industry, and it stilmakes a small sum from selling arms abroad. lt also trades servicesaccountancy (会计业)，insurance, banking and advertising. The governmenibelieves Britain is on the cutting edge of the knowledge economy. After all, thecountry of Shakespeare and Wordsworth has a literary tradition of which to beproud. Rock 'n' roll is an English language medium, and there are billions to bemade by their cutting-edge bands. in other words. the creative economy hasplenty of strength to carry the British economy.
However, creative industries account for only about four percent of UK's exports otgoods and services. The industries are finding it hard to make a profit, accordincto a report of the National Endowment for Science, Technology and the Arts. Thereport shows only 38 percent of British companies were engaged in “innovatioractivities”, three percentage points below the EU average and well below Germany(61 percent) and Sweden (47 percent).
in fact, it might be better to call Britain a “servant” economy - there are at leastfour million people “in service”. The majority of the population is employed by therich to cook, clean, and take care of their children. Many graduates are even doingmenial (小人的) jobs for which they do not need a degree. Most employmentgrowth has been, and will continue to be, at the low-skill end of the service sector- in shops, bars, hotels, domestic service and in nursing and care homes.
这是原文，请你回答下面五道题
11. What can be learnt from the first paragraph?
A. Technology plays a role in today's knowledgeeconomy for every country.
B. Every country has its own contribution to today'sknowledge economy.
C. Knowledge is important for a country's survival.
D. A country has to offer its best products to survivein the knowledge economy
12.Why do some officials say that Britain has nothing toworry about although it suffers a heavy trade deficit?
A. Because they believe Britain is keeping up withthe knowledge economy.
B. Because Britain is still making profit from sometrade services.
C.Because the creative literary tradition has strengthto carry the British economy.
D. Because they believe Britain still has some world-class industries.
13.Why were there fewer British companies engaged ininnovation activities”?
A.Because British companies are not good atinnovation activities
B.Because creative industries are difficult to makemoney.
C. Because the competition between differenteconomies is fierce.
D.Because Britain can rely on other industries tomake profits.
14. What does the author mean by saying Britain'seconomy a “servant” one?
A. Many people are employed to do low-skill jobs.B. Many people are willing to be servantsC. Many people do not have the ability to earn adegree.
D. Many people do not have creative ideas15.What is probably the author's attitude toward UK's
15. What is probably the author's attitude toward UK'scurrent economy?
A. Sneering.
B. Confident.
C. Sympathetic.
D. Doubtful.

根据提供的文段，以下是我对这五个问题的回答：

11. What can be learnt from the first paragraph?
答案: B. Every country has its own contribution to today's knowledge economy.
解析: 第一段提到了不同国家在知识经济中擅长的不同领域，说明每个国家都有其独特的贡献。

12. Why do some officials say that Britain has nothing to worry about although it suffers a heavy trade deficit?
答案: A. Because they believe Britain is keeping up with the knowledge economy.
解析: 尽管英国有较大的贸易赤字，但官员们认为无需担心，是因为他们认为英国正在跟上知识经济的发展。

13. Why were there fewer British companies engaged in innovation activities?
答案: B. Because creative industries are difficult to make money.
解析: 文中提到创意行业很难盈利，并且英国公司参与创新活动的比例低于欧盟平均水平和其他一些国家。

14. What does the author mean by saying Britain's economy a “servant” one?
答案: A. Many people are employed to do low-skill jobs.
解析: 作者称英国经济为“服务业”经济，意味着大量人口从事低技能的服务工作。

15. What is probably the author's attitude toward UK's current economy?
答案: D. Doubtful.
解析: 作者对于创意行业仅占出口的小部分和许多人从事低端服务工作的情况提出了批判，这表明他/她对英国目前的经济状况持有怀疑态度。

Believe it or not, the Swiss were once a warlike people. But the Swiss discoveredlong ago that constant warfare (争) brought them nothing but suffering and povertyBy adopting a policy of neutrality, Switzerland, a country, with hardly any naturaresources, enjoyed peace and prosperity. The rest of the world is still not ready tcaccept this simple and obvious solution. Most countries not only maintain permaneniarmies but require all their young men to do a period of compulsory military serviceEverybody has a lot to say about the desirability of peace, but no one does anythingabout it. An obvious thing to do would be to abolish conscription (征兵) everywhereThis would be the first step towards universal peace
Some countries, like Britain, have already abandoned peace-time conscriptionUnfortunately, they haven't done so for idealistic reasons, but a simple recognition otthe fact that modern warfare is a highly professional business. In the old days largearmies were essential. But in these days of inter-continental missiles and push-buttorwarfare. unskilled manpower has become redundant (多余的). In a mere two vears oiso, you can't hope to train soldiers in the requirement and conditions of modernwarfare. So why bother? Leave it to the professionals!
There are also pressing personal reasons to abolish conscription. it is mostunpleasant in times of peace for young men to grow up under the threat of militarservice. They are deprived of two of the best and most formative years of their livesTheir careers and studies are seriously affected and sometimes the whole course ottheir lives is altered. They spend at least two years in the armed forces engaged inactivities which do not provide them with any useful experience with regard to theirfuture work. lt can't even be argued that what they learn might prove valuable in anational emergency. When they leave the services, young men quickly forget all theunnecessary information about warfare which they were made to acquire. lt isshocking to think that skilled and unskilled men are often nothing more than a sourceof cheap labor for the military.
这是原文，请你回答下面五道题：
16.What do we know about the Swiss?
O A. They are warlike people and maintain a permanentarmy.
B. They live in a country that hardly has any naturalresources
C.They enjoyed peace and prosperity thanks to theircompulsory military service
D. They found that neutrality would bring them onlysuffering and poverty.
17.The reason Britain abandoned peace-time conscriptionis that
A. skilled manpower is insufficient in Britain
B. Britain recognized the importance of this step forworld peace
C.modern warfare needs professionals
D. Britain planned to give up its permanent army
18. One of the pressing personal reasons to abolishconscription is that
 A. young men are not motivated to join the army intimes of peace
 B. young men's course of lives will possibly bechanged
C. the two year military service is under the threat ofwars
D. young men will quickly forget all the necessaryinformation about warfare
19.Which of the following can be inferred from the
passage?
A. Young men can get some experience for theirfuture work from military service.B Most countries are not ready to abandon theirpermanent armies.
C. The size of an army is still very importantnowadays
D. More and more countries are adopting the policyof neutrality.
20. The best title for this text is A.No need to maintain permanent armies B. Uniformity and conformity in military service C.Why abolish conscription
D.Peace and prosperity in Switzerland

根据提供的文段，以下是我对这五个问题的回答：

16. What do we know about the Swiss?
答案: B. They live in a country that hardly has any natural resources.
解析: 文中指出瑞士是一个几乎没有任何自然资源的国家，但通过采取中立政策，享受了和平与繁荣。

17. The reason Britain abandoned peace-time conscription is that
答案: C. modern warfare needs professionals.
解析: 文中指出英国废除和平时期的征兵制不是出于理想主义原因，而是因为现代战争高度专业化。

18. One of the pressing personal reasons to abolish conscription is that
答案: B. young men's course of lives will possibly be changed.
解析: 文中提到征兵制使年轻人失去了生活中最好和最具有形成作用的两年时间，影响他们的职业和学习，并有可能改变他们生活的整个进程。

19. Which of the following can be inferred from the passage?
答案: B. Most countries are not ready to abandon their permanent armies.
解析: 文中提到大多数国家不仅保持着常备军，而且要求所有年轻人服一定期限的义务军事服务，因此可以推断出大多数国家还未准备好放弃他们的常备军队。

20. The best title for this text is
答案: C. Why abolish conscription.
解析: 全文讨论的是为什么应该废除征兵制，和平与繁荣、维护常备军队的必要性以及其他相关议题，最佳标题是C：反映了文中讨论的主要议题。

Many immigrants who settled in the United States were poor. The country they cameto was a wilderness. Land had to be cleared of trees in order to make farms; mineshad to be developed; houses, shops, and public buildings had to be built. Everyoneendeavored to earn their livelihood. Manual labor was highly valued. Thereafter itwas the man who worked with his head to achieve success in business and industrwho was esteemed. Now there is in America a curious combination of pride in havinorisen to a position where it is no longer necessary to depend on manual labor for aliving and genuine delight in what one is able to accomplish with one's own hands.
The attitude toward manual labor is seen in an array of things in American life. One isinvited to dinner at the home of a middle-aged couple. lt is not only comfortable butextravagantly furnished together with beautifully landscaped gardens: vet the hostessprobably will cook and serve the dinner. Additionally, she is prone to do much of thehousework, and even though the husband may be a professional man, he talks aboutwashing the car, digging in the flowerbeds, or painting the house. He often assistsher in washing dishes and doing other household chores (杂务) aptly. The son odaughter who is away at college may get a summer job involving manual labor tcearn next vear's school expense
lt has been an American ideal to rise from a humble beginning to a better position.Therefore the "servant class has not remained a fixed group. ln fact, it has almostceased to exist because people, who in earlier days might have been servants, nowget higher wages working in factories. The majority of families cannot afford to paywhat people who do housework or gardening charge for their services
The expense of household service and of skilled labor, such as painting anocarpentry. and the tradition of working with one's hands have contributed to keepincalive the spirit of “do-it-yourself”. Many articles in popular magazines are developedto instruction in gardening, carpentry, and interior decorating. The average Americargets a good deal of pleasure out of telling others about his or her projects, exhibitinothem for their neighbors, or taking them to the country fair, where they will surely wina prize.
这是原文，请你回答下面五道题：
21. According to the first paragraph, the Americans
A. only took pride in achieving success in businessand industry in the past
B.only valued manual labor in the past
C. are more proud of being successful in workingwith head now
D. still take great delight in manual labor
22.From the passage we can learn that people who dohousework or gardening
A are poorly paid
B.are less respected than those who work with head
C.are well paid
D.are looked down upon
23.According to the passage, which of the followingstatements is true?
A. The salary for housework and gardening is so highthat attracts many people
B AlI Americans rise from a humble beginning to abetter position.
C.In America, there is no servant class
24. According to the passage, the American attitude towardmanuallabor is
A. indifferent
B. negative
C. neutral
D.positive
25.From the passage we can infer that Americans arewealthy because
A. they are more skilled than othersB. they are more intelligent than othersC. they have made it with their own handsD. they often work with head
D. College students have to do manual labor to earntheir school expense.

根据提供的文段，以下是我对这五个问题的回答：

21. According to the first paragraph, the Americans
答案: D. still take great delight in manual labor.
解析: 第一段讨论了美国人对手工劳动的看法演变，特别提到当前美国人虽然以不再依赖手工劳动为生为荣，但仍然对亲手完成的工作感到快乐。

22. From the passage we can learn that people who do housework or gardening
答案: C. are well paid.
解析: 文章提到多数家庭无法负担做家务或园艺者所收取的服务费，这暗示这些工作的报酬是相对不错的。

23. According to the passage, which of the following statements is true?
答案: C. In America, there is no servant class.
解析: 文中提到“‘服务者阶层’并没有成为一个固定的群体，实际上几乎已经停止存在，因为早先可能成为服务者的人现在在工厂里能获得更高的工资。”

24. According to the passage, the American attitude toward manual labor is
答案: D. positive.
解析: 从文中可以看出美国人对手工劳动的态度是积极的，他们自豪于能够自己动手完成活儿，并乐于谈论和展示自己的项目。

25. From the passage we can infer that Americans are wealthy because
答案: C. they have made it with their own hands.
解析: 文章中没有提到美国人之所以富有是由于他们比其他人更有技能或智能，而是强调了自己动手完成工作的价值，并描述了人们通过“自己动手”的精神获取快乐，因此C选项与文中内容相符。
